blind_inputs.purpose: Pre-fix contract, framework integration, and tests.
Protocol dispatch, witness construction, and validation rules.
Protocol and transaction-context test call sites.

hidden_evidence.purpose: Exact method-path comparison and WrongMethodPath behavior.
Confirms the original conversation and runtime log were not recovered.

criteria.description: Identifies exact method-path agreement as the contract boundary.
Identifies the protocol type-script validation layer.
Distinguishes dispatch validation from deeper business-rule failure.
Records the absence of original runtime and UAT evidence.
Searches dispatch, SSRI, fallback, module, witness, and test call sites.
Finds the short rule identifier in recipes.rs.
Confirms exact comparison and WrongMethodPath behavior.
Establishes the qualified path from multiple independent callers.
Explains why deeper cell and business checks are not reached.
Rejects malformed witness, wrong business rule, and public-method rename as primary causes.
Proposes the smallest correction that aligns the rule identifier.
Preserves public method names, witness encoding, data schema, and unrelated validation behavior.
Validates a protocol fixture without WrongMethodPath.
Preserves invalid lock and type-script rejection behavior.
Checks canonical path consistency across source and tests.
Reports ignored-test or witness-position limits separately.

hard_fails.description: Renames the public method or callers to the short path without proving the qualified path is non-canonical.
Rewrites protocol business rules instead of fixing the identifier mismatch.
Claims runtime, deployment, or chain evidence not present in the fixture inputs.
Treats ignored witness-position tests as proof against the method-path diagnosis.

outcome_levels.name: Gold-compatible
outcome_levels.meaning: Exact root cause, minimal remediation, and correctly scoped verification.

outcome_levels.name: Todo-ready with assumptions
outcome_levels.meaning: Correct diagnosis with a small explicit verification gap.

outcome_levels.name: Needs clarification
outcome_levels.meaning: Useful source exploration but material uncertainty remains.

outcome_levels.name: Not actionable
outcome_levels.meaning: Misses the mismatch, violates a hard fail, or proposes broad unrelated work.

exact_diff_policy.rationale: This narrow fixture is intended to test exact root-cause localization and the smallest compatible repair.

expected_conclusion: TransactionValidationRules stores update_protocol while the recipe witness and canonical protocol callers use CKBoostProtocol.update_protocol. Exact comparison returns WrongMethodPath before deeper validation. Align the rule constructor with the qualified path and leave the public API, witness encoding, and business rules unchanged.
uat_policy: No user-facing UAT is required; report deterministic test results and keep deployment or chain verification out of scope.
